assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 77: 'Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate, 2 regressions); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56). Repetitive Test Detection: N/A. Test Documentation: N/A.'

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-12: 'Eval result review detected: Review ID 40001 from github-actions[bot]' with all three detection criteria confirmed (author, ## Eval Results marker, sdlc-workflow/run-evals footer). Eval metrics table shows per-eval pass rates including eval-3 at 85%.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md: Classification is 'suggestion'. Section 'Not an eval result' explicitly checks all 3 eval detection criteria and confirms all FAIL (author is reviewer-b not github-actions[bot], no ## Eval Results marker, no sdlc-workflow/run-evals footer). Classification reasoning references suggestive language ('Consider adding'), not eval metrics.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 77 Test Quality Details: 'Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate, 2 regressions)'. report.md lines 28-33 detail the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation with their evidence text.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md exists and targets eval-3 assertion failures. Description: 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures in the verify-pr skill. Two assertions are failing at an 85% pass rate'. Review Context section quotes both failing assertion texts verbatim.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md Review Context section (lines 39-49): Quotes Failing Assertion 1 about convention upgrade eligibility with evidence 'review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility', and Failing Assertion 2 about sub-task creation with evidence 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 36: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 70: 'Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Eval failure sub-task created for eval-3; root-cause investigation performed on convention upgrade eligibility gap'. Lines 86-88 provide a Root-Cause Investigation Summary describing the method-based skill gap classification.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 11 output files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md, review-50001.md, subtask-1.md) are analytical markdown documents. None contain code modifications, git commands, or file write operations targeting source code.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md: Scope Containment PASS ('PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md'), Sensitive Patterns PASS ('No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected'), Acceptance Criteria PASS ('7 of 7 criteria met'). Each is evaluated based on diff content and task specification, independent of eval processing.
